input.user_input: Add a force majeure clause to this supply agreement. Use the ISDA standard definition as a starting point.

actual:
It looks like you didn't attach or upload the supply agreement. Please upload the document, and I will be happy to insert the clause for you. 

In the meantime, here is a draft of a Force Majeure clause adapted for a commercial supply agreement, using the core concepts from the ISDA Master Agreement's "Force Majeure Event" definition (specifically focusing on impossibility/impracticability, events beyond reasonable control, and the requirement to use all reasonable efforts to overcome the event):

**[X]. Force Majeure**

**(a) Force Majeure Event.** For the purposes of this Agreement, a "Force Majeure Event" means any event or circumstance (including, without limitation, any act of State, governmental action, natural disaster, war, strike, or labor disruption) that is beyond the reasonable control of the affected Party, and which makes it impossible or impracticable for such Party to perform its obligations under this Agreement (other than an obligation to make a payment of money), provided that such Party could not, after using all reasonable efforts (which will not require such Party to incur a loss, other than immaterial, incidental expenses), overcome such impossibility or impracticability.

**(b) Suspension of Performance.** If a Party is prevented from or delayed in performing any of its obligations under this Agreement by a Force Majeure Event (the "Affected Party"), the Affected Party shall not be in breach of this Agreement or otherwise liable for any such failure or delay in the performance of such obligations. The time for performance of such obligations shall be extended accordingly.

**(c) Notice Requirement.** The Affected Party shall promptly notify the other Party in writing of the occurrence of the Force Majeure Event, the nature of the event, and the anticipated duration of the delay or impossibility of performance. The Affected Party shall continue to use all reasonable efforts to mitigate the effects of the Force Majeure Event and resume performance as soon as reasonably
